Recognition

Achievements

Research awards Competitive programming Academic distinction
Highlights

What stands out

Research impact Recognized at both national and departmental level for final-year research work.
Consistent performance Strong results across programming contests, hackathons, and academic milestones.
Timeline

Awards and recognitions

2025 Research recognition
Manamperi Award (Engineering) - Merit Award

Awarded to an outstanding undergraduate engineering research project carried out at a faculty of engineering in a Sri Lankan university or higher education institute.

2025 Departmental award
Prof. W.P. Jayasekara Prize

Awarded for the best final year research project in Electrical and Electronic Engineering at the University of Peradeniya.

2023 and 2024 Competitive programming
IEEEXtreme Global Top 100 for Two Consecutive Years

Placed in the global top 100 in IEEEXtreme 17.0, 18.0, and 19.0, becoming part of the first team from Sri Lanka to sustain that achievement across three consecutive years.

2024 Competitive programming
IEEEXtreme 18.0

Achieved Global Rank 66 and Country Rank 01 in Sri Lanka.

2023 Competitive programming
IEEEXtreme 17.0

Achieved Global Rank 65 and Country Rank 01 in Sri Lanka.

2024 Competitive programming
Coders v11.0 All Island Competitive Programming Competition

Champions.

2024 Hackathon
Realhack 5.0 Islandwide Hackathon

2nd runners-up.

2024 Competitive programming
Cypher 2.0 All Island Competitive Programming Competition

2nd runners-up.

2023 Competitive programming
MoraXtreme 8.0 All Island Competitive Programming Competition

Champions.

2023 Competitive programming
Coders v10.0 All Island Competitive Programming Competition

1st runners-up.

2022 Competitive programming
Coders v9.0 All Island Competitive Programming Competition

Champions.

2022 University competition
Shantha Bandara Inter-Faculty Debate Competition

Champions.
